quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 9 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: Defining and Identifying Basic Parts of a Circle
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 9.1 Circles, Radii, Chords, and Diameters

questions.question: A circle is defined as:
options: The set of all points in a plane that are equidistant from a fixed center point
A closed quadrilateral with four congruent sides and four congruent right angles
The set of all points in a plane whose distances to two fixed foci sum to a constant
A theoretical polygon with an infinite number of sides that approximates a curve
questions.explanation: A circle is the set of all points in a plane that are a fixed distance (the radius) from a fixed point (the center). This definition is fundamental to understanding all circle theorems and properties.

questions.question: A radius of a circle is a segment that:
options: Connects the center to any point on the circle
Connects any two points on the circle
Passes through the circle without touching it
Connects two points on the circle and passes through the center
questions.explanation: A radius connects the center of the circle to any point on the circle. All radii of the same circle are congruent (equal in length). The plural of radius is radii.

questions.question: A chord of a circle is a segment that:
options: Connects any two points on the circle
Always passes through the center
Connects the center to a point on the circle
Is always perpendicular to a radius
questions.explanation: A chord is a segment whose endpoints both lie on the circle. A diameter is a special chord that passes through the center. Every diameter is a chord, but not every chord is a diameter.

questions.question: A diameter of a circle is:
options: A chord that passes through the center of the circle
Any line that touches the circle at exactly one point
A segment from the center to any point on the circle
The distance around the circle
questions.explanation: A diameter is a chord that passes through the center of the circle. Its length is twice the radius: $d = 2r$. The diameter is the longest possible chord in a circle.

questions.question: If a circle has a radius of $7$ cm, what is the length of its diameter?
options: $7$ cm
questions.explanation: The diameter is twice the radius: $d = 2r = 2 \times 7 = 14$ cm. All diameters of a circle are congruent.

questions.question: If a circle has a diameter of $18$ inches, what is the length of its radius?
options: $9$ inches
$36$ inches
$18$ inches
$6$ inches
questions.explanation: The radius is half the diameter: $r = \frac{d}{2} = \frac{18}{2} = 9$ inches. The radius is the distance from the center to any point on the circle.

questions.question: In a circle, how many radii can be drawn from the center?
options: Infinitely many
Exactly two
Exactly four
One for every point on the diameter
questions.explanation: Since there are infinitely many points on a circle, there are infinitely many radii. Every radius of a given circle is congruent to every other radius of that circle. This property is fundamental to many circle proofs.

questions.question: Which statement about chords is true?
options: The longest chord in a circle is the diameter
All chords are equal in length
A chord cannot be longer than the radius
Chords always intersect at the center
questions.explanation: The diameter is the longest possible chord in a circle because it passes through the center. Any other chord is shorter than the diameter. A chord's length can range from just above $0$ up to $2r$ (the diameter).

questions.question: In $\odot O$, points $A$ and $B$ lie on the circle. $\overline{OA}$ and $\overline{OB}$ are radii. If $OA = 5x - 3$ and $OB = 2x + 9$, what is the radius of the circle?
questions.explanation: All radii of a circle are congruent, so $OA = OB$. Set up: $5x - 3 = 2x + 9$, giving $3x = 12$ and $x = 4$. Then radius $= 5(4) - 3 = 20 - 3 = 17$. Check: $2(4) + 9 = 8 + 9 = 17$. Both radii equal $17$.

questions.question: In a circle, a segment has one endpoint at the center and the other on the circle. This segment is best described as:
options: A radius
A diameter
A chord
An arc
questions.explanation: A segment from the center to any point on the circle is, by definition, a radius. If it continued through the center to the other side of the circle, it would be a diameter. Both of its endpoints are not on the circle, so it is not a chord.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the basic parts of a circle — center, radius, chord, and diameter — and understand the relationships between them.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Remember: a radius goes from center to circle, a chord connects two points on the circle, and a diameter is a special chord that passes through the center ($d = 2r$). All radii of a circle are congruent.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: (1) radius = center to any point on the circle, (2) chord = segment with both endpoints on the circle, (3) diameter = chord through the center, (4) $d = 2r$ and $r = \frac{d}{2}$.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! A circle is all points equidistant from a center. A radius connects the center to the circle. A chord connects two points on the circle. A diameter is a chord through the center and equals twice the radius. All radii of the same circle are equal.
